text.source_text: यत्तूक्तं सत्त्वधर्मेण ज्ञानेन सर्वज्ञं प्रधानं भविष्यतीति , तन्नोपपद्यते । न हि प्रधानावस्थायां गुणसाम्यात्सत्त्वधर्मो ज्ञानं सम्भवति । ननूक्तं सर्वज्ञानशक्तिमत्त्वेन सर्वज्ञं भविष्यतीति ; तदपि नोपपद्यते । यदि गुणसाम्ये सति सत्त्वव्यपाश्रयां ज्ञानशक्तिमाश्रित्य सर्वज्ञं प्रधानमुच्येत , कामं रजस्तमोव्यपाश्रयामपि ज्ञानप्रतिबन्धकशक्तिमाश्रित्य किञ्चिज्ज्ञमुच्येत । अपि च नासाक्षिका सत्त्ववृत्तिर्जानातिना अभिधीयते । न चाचेतनस्य प्रधानस्य साक्षित्वमस्ति । तस्मादनुपपन्नं प्रधानस्य सर्वज्ञत्वम् । योगिनां तु चेतनत्वात्सत्त्वोत्कर्षनिमित्तं सर्वज्ञत्वमुपपन्नमित्यनुदाहरणम् । अथ पुनः साक्षिनिमित्तमीक्षितृत्वं प्रधानस्य कल्प्येत , यथाग्निनिमित्तमयःपिण्डादेर्दग्धृत्वम् । तथा सति यन्निमित्तमीक्षितृत्वं प्रधानस्य , तदेव सर्वज्ञं ब्रह्म मुख्यं जगतः कारणमिति युक्तम् । यत्पुनरुक्तं ब्रह्मणोऽपि न मुख्यं सर्वज्ञत्वमुपपद्यते , नित्यज्ञानक्रियत्वे ज्ञानक्रियां प्रति स्वातन्त्र्यासम्भवादिति । अत्रोच्यते — इदं ताव द्भवान्प्रष्टव्यः — कथं नित्यज्ञानक्रियत्वे सर्वज्ञत्वहानिरिति । यस्य हि सर्वविषयावभासनक्षमं ज्ञानं नित्यमस्ति , सोऽसर्वज्ञ इति विप्रतिषिद्धम् । अनित्यत्वे हि ज्ञानस्य , कदाचिज्जानाति कदाचिन्न जानातीत्यसर्वज्ञत्वमपि स्यात् ।
text.transliteration_iast: yattūktaṃ sattvadharmeṇa jñānena sarvajñaṃ pradhānaṃ bhaviṣyatīti , tannopapadyate | na hi pradhānāvasthāyāṃ guṇasāmyātsattvadharmo jñānaṃ sambhavati | nanūktaṃ sarvajñānaśaktimattvena sarvajñaṃ bhaviṣyatīti ; tadapi nopapadyate | yadi guṇasāmye sati sattvavyapāśrayāṃ jñānaśaktimāśritya sarvajñaṃ pradhānamucyeta , kāmaṃ rajastamovyapāśrayāmapi jñānapratibandhakaśaktimāśritya kiñcijjñamucyeta | api ca nāsākṣikā sattvavṛttirjānātinā abhidhīyate | na cācetanasya pradhānasya sākṣitvamasti | tasmādanupapannaṃ pradhānasya sarvajñatvam | yogināṃ tu cetanatvātsattvotkarṣanimittaṃ sarvajñatvamupapannamityanudāharaṇam | atha punaḥ sākṣinimittamīkṣitṛtvaṃ pradhānasya kalpyeta , yathāgninimittamayaḥpiṇḍāderdagdhṛtvam | tathā sati yannimittamīkṣitṛtvaṃ pradhānasya , tadeva sarvajñaṃ brahma mukhyaṃ jagataḥ kāraṇamiti yuktam | yatpunaruktaṃ brahmaṇo'pi na mukhyaṃ sarvajñatvamupapadyate , nityajñānakriyatve jñānakriyāṃ prati svātantryāsambhavāditi | atrocyate — idaṃ tāva dbhavānpraṣṭavyaḥ — kathaṃ nityajñānakriyatve sarvajñatvahāniriti | yasya hi sarvaviṣayāvabhāsanakṣamaṃ jñānaṃ nityamasti , so'sarvajña iti vipratiṣiddham | anityatve hi jñānasya , kadācijjānāti kadācinna jānātītyasarvajñatvamapi syāt |

translation.literal_translation: As for the claim that Pradhana may still be called omniscient through sattva's knowledge-quality, this is untenable. In Pradhana-state, with gunas in equilibrium, sattva's knowledge function does not arise. If one says it is omniscient by possessing the power for knowledge, that too fails: if in guna-equilibrium one invokes sattva-based knowledge-power to call it omniscient, one could equally invoke rajas/tamas-based obstruction-power and call it only partially knowing. Further, knowing is spoken of only where there is witness-awareness of sattva-modification; inert Pradhana has no witnessing capacity. Hence Pradhana's omniscience is impossible. Yogins are no example, because being conscious they can be omniscient through sattva-excellence. If one instead imagines Pradhana's seeing to be derivative from some witness, as iron burns by fire-contact, then that very witness-ground is the truly omniscient Brahman and the principal world-cause. As for saying Brahman's omniscience cannot be primary because if knowledge is eternal it has no freedom toward acts of knowing: this objection should first explain how eternal knowledge negates omniscience. One who eternally has knowledge capable of illuminating all objects cannot be non-omniscient. If knowledge were non-eternal, then sometimes knowing and sometimes not would indeed imply non-omniscience.
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya continues 1.1.5 by dismantling guna-based Pradhana omniscience and defending nitya-jnana as compatible with, and required for, sarvajnatva.
translation.interpretive_note: Turns the opponent's power-language into an argument for conscious witness-causality.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Used to rebut claim that Pradhana could host operative omniscient cognition in causal state.
Demonstrates mismatch between inert equilibrium and all-knowing causality.
Bhashya denies that mere potentiality rescues Pradhana from acetanatva objection.
Prepared as positive model for Ishvara’s omniscience against episodic cognition accounts.
Reinforces that jagat-cause requires sentience, not matter-equilibrium.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S01 pass with stable inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit long compound argument boundaries and technical Mimamsa-Sankhya polemic vocabulary in records 1.1.024-1.1.031.
